Sancho.additionalHistoryText: Web research (verify): Visual artist/illustrator; journey rooted in Birmingham and Shanghai street contexts; IT/design background. Collaborators named on site include NBCUniversal, LG, Wrike, Revolut, Carlsberg, LOQI, and others (verify). Distribution via Gunter Gallery Madrid, Wallbaby Stockholm, Saatchi Art, Inprnt (per site/about summaries).
Sancho.exhibitionsText:
2023 — Recognition, Saatchi Art Best of June (online/feature)
2022 — Group, Boomer Gallery, London, UK
2022 — Award context, The Heartbeat of Macao, Macao (jury award; verify category)
2022 — Grant, Fine Acts x University of California (verify)

Alice Bureau.additionalHistoryText: Web research (verify): Alice Hoffmann, working as Bureau Alice / Alice Bureau, Zurich-based illustrator and art director; German-born, describes strong ties to Spain. 20+ years in international agency creative leadership before pivot to illustration (~15 years AD then illustration focus). Clients cited include Meta, Instagram, Adobe, Diptyque, UBS, Illy, Swiss Post stamps, Forno Brisa packaging (verify on portfolio).

Alin Mor.additionalHistoryText: Web research (verify): Haifa-based illustrator; Bezalel BA visual communications; freelance since 2016. Murals for offices and public buildings; themes include femininity, archetypes, spirituality, nature. Collaborators cited include Meta Israel, Suzuki Israel, She Shreds; press mentions Elephant, Haaretz (verify).
Alin Mor.exhibitionsText:
2022 — Group, Culture of Solidarity, Terder.fm, Tel Aviv, Israel
2021 — Group, The Pink Period, Israeli Illustration Week, Jaffa Museum, Jaffa, Israel
2019 — Group, Women, Israeli Illustration Week, Teder, Tel Aviv, Israel
2019 — Workshop show, The Blue Period, Israeli Illustration Week, Tel Aviv, Israel
2018 — Group, One Dot Will Never Rule, RVCA Workshop, Tel Aviv, Israel
2017 — Solo, Jungle, Haifa, Israel
2017 — Group, Israeli Illustration Week, Abraham Hostel, Israel
2017 — Group, The Other Walls, Shaar 3 Gallery, Haifa, Israel

Beto Val.additionalHistoryText: Web research (verify): Beto Valencia (Beto Val), Quito-based Ecuadorian digital collage artist (@elbetoval). Practice expanded during COVID-era experimentation with public-domain vintage illustration. Surreal, whimsical worlds; themes include nature and fantasy. Presence on Rubin Museum artist pages, iCanvas, Artinrug (verify licensing claims).

Designer Daily — — Digital collage feature — https://www.designer-daily.com/beto-vals-digital-collages-are-pure-poetry-134806
Rubin Museum — — People / artist listing — https://rubinmuseum.org/people/beto-val/
Artinrug — — Artist page — https://artinrug.com/artist/beto-val/

Cokorda Martin.additionalHistoryText: Web research (verify): Balinese illustrator, b. Klungkung 1995; career from 2013. Fusion of Balinese, Japanese, Chinese myth with pop color; represented by Debut Art (linktree). Collaborations: Uniqlo Indonesia, Café Kitsuné Paris, Jägermeister Indonesia, Mossery, Ana-tomy, Rhino Shield (verify).

Hen Macabi.additionalHistoryText: Web research (verify): Israeli graphic designer/illustrator; Shanker (Shenkar) graduate 2013 per shop bios. Music packaging and brand illustration; known public project: artwork for Netta Barzilai “Bassa Sababa” (Haaretz 2019). AlefAlefAlef project: graphic tribute to 70 years of Israeli cinema (VHS-cover aesthetic).
